concerns.text: Task T012 (runner.py) and T014 (metrics.py) consume the metrics schema defined in T006. However, T006 is marked [P] (parallel) alongside T004/T005. If T012/T014 start before T006 completes, they will fail to validate against a non-existent schema. T006 must be a strict prerequisite (non-parallel) for all US1 implementation tasks.

Task T020 (resource_monitor.py) and T021 (refine runner.py) depend on the timeout wrapper mechanism established in T008. T008 is in Phase 2 (Foundational), but T020/T021 are in Phase 4 (US2). The dependency is implied but the parallel marker [P] on T008 creates a race condition risk if US2 tasks are scheduled before T008 finishes. T008 must be explicitly ordered before T020/T021.

Task T026 (analyzer.py) and T027 (reporter.py) consume the structured metrics artifacts generated by T014 (metrics.py). T014 is in Phase 3 (US1), while T026/T027 are in Phase 5 (US3). While phases are sequential, the [P] tag on T014 implies it could run in parallel with other US1 tasks. If T026/T027 are triggered before T014 produces the artifact, they fail. The flow must enforce T014 completion before T026/T027 start.

Task T009 (dataset fetcher) is marked [P] (parallel) in Phase 2. However, it is a foundational data source for US1, US2, and US3. If T009 runs in parallel with T004 (config) or T005 (utils) and fails (e.g., network timeout), downstream tasks might assume data availability. While [P] is technically safe for file isolation, the semantic dependency on 'data availability' suggests it should be a blocking step before any US implementation begins, not just a parallel setup task.
